name: Ekaitza and Arritzulegi Reservoir Loop
name_original: Cima de Ekaitza – circular desde Saldias por el Embalse de Arritzegi
description: This difficult 19.8-mile mountain biking route from Saldias offers panoramic views from Ekaitza Summit and passes the Arritzulegi Reservoir.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Saldías traverse a landscape characterized by forested areas and reservoir surroundings. The region features varied terrain suitable for mountain biking, with routes that include ascents and descents. Mountain bikers can explore paths that wind through natural settings, offering diverse riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Saldías The most popular mountain bike trail is Leurtza Reservoirs - Txaruta Summit Loop, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a mix of terrain around the reservoirs and towards a summit. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ekaitza and Arritzulegi Reservoir Loop, a difficult 19.8 miles (31.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, winding around two distinct reservoirs. Local mountain bikers also love the Martyconea loop from Basaburua, a 14.9 miles (24.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Mountain biking around Saldías is defined by forested paths, reservoir loops, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Saldías are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Saldías's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Saldías?
A: The mountain bike trails around Saldías offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find paths winding through forested areas and around reservoirs, with varied elevation changes including both ascents and descents. The region provides options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult, ensuring a dynamic riding experience.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Saldías area?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails available for exploration around Saldías. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 4 easy, 17 moderate, and 16 difficult options to choose from.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Saldías?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Saldías are designed as loops. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990021984/leurtza-reservoirs-txaruta-summit-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leurtza Reservoirs - Txaruta Summit Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 15.8-mile ride, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990027523/ekaitza-and-arritzulegi-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ekaitza and Arritzulegi Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 19.8 miles with significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can tackle routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990027523/ekaitza-and-arritzulegi-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ekaitza and Arritzulegi Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 19.8-mile path with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest – Puerto Otzola loop from Beruete&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 31.1-mile route.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Saldías?
A: While mountain biking around Saldías, you can encounter several interesting natural features and landmarks. The trails often pass by the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leurtza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You might also discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3604192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Putzubeltza Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5653747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pittortzar Dolmen&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any facilities or picnic areas along the trails?
A: Yes, you can find facilities such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3566434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Fountain and Picnic Area in the Forest&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a convenient spot to rest and refuel during your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Saldías?
A: The mountain bike routes in Saldías are highly rated by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic forested paths, and the engaging reservoir loops.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike route to try in Saldías?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martyconea loop from Jauntsarats&lt;/a&gt;. This 12.1-mile trail offers a balanced ride with manageable elevation changes, making it suitable for those looking for a less strenuous but still engaging experience.

Q: Can I find any historical sites or interesting structures near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3860943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doneztebe (Santesteban) Historic Centre&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3860829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bidasoa Dam and Sluice Gates&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable man-made structures in the area.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling paths that connect with the Saldías trails?
A: While the guide focuses on local mountain bike trails, the region is known for its interconnected networks. You can find sections of greenways like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5199349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway (Lekunberri–Leitza)&lt;/a&gt; nearby, offering additional cycling opportunities.

Q: What is the typical duration for a mountain bike ride in Saldías?
A: Ride durations vary depending on the trail's length and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990021984/leurtza-reservoirs-txaruta-summit-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leurtza Reservoirs - Txaruta Summit Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 46 minutes, while a more challenging 19.8-mile loop could take closer to 4 hours.
tips: Easy climb, bearing for the top and a bit on the way down, it is worth going up for the views and tranquility.
Small reservoir located in a beautiful and picturesque environment between the typical forest and mountain landscape of the Navarrese interior, a good place to take a break and regain energy.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Cima de Ekaitza mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult for mountain biking. It requires a very good physical condition and experience. The terrain includes demanding ascents, a spectacular crest with earthy and some rocky sections, and a descent from the summit. Some portions may even require you to push your bike, corresponding to S2-S6 levels on the Single Trail Scale.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain, including tracks, demanding ascents, and flat areas. The route passes through forests and features a notable 'spectacular crest' on the way to the Ekaitza Summit, which is primarily an earthy path but includes some brief rocky sections. There's also a significant descent from the summit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cima de Ekaitza mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Since the trail is circular and starts and ends near Saldias, you can typically find parking in or around the village of Saldias. Specific parking areas for trail access are usually available, but it's always good to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What are the best viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The most rewarding views are from the Ekaitza Summit, which offers beautiful panoramic vistas. You can also expect formidable panoramic views of the Malerreka Valley and the Arritzulegi Reservoir from various points along the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Cima de Ekaitza – circular desde Saldias por el Embalse de Arritzegi trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the region, mountain biking with a dog on a difficult route like this requires careful consideration. The varied terrain, demanding climbs, and potential for rocky sections might be challenging for some dogs. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially near wildlife or other trail users, and ensure they are fit enough for the distance and elevation.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to mountain bike on this route in the Navarra region. However, it's always a good idea to check the official Visit Navarra website (visitnavarra.es) for any updated regulations or local advisories before your trip.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Navarra region generally offers good conditions for mountain biking outside of peak winter. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so starting early in the day is recommended if riding during warmer months. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of trails such as Bustitza - Uztakorta, Aurkidiko itzulia, and various segments of Uraren bidea, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the Cima de Ekaitza route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The main highlights include reaching the Ekaitza Summit, which features a large stone tower and a flag, and riding around the scenic Arritzulegi Reservoir. The entire route offers stunning natural scenery, including idyllic oak and beech woods.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating and duration, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate protective gear including a helmet. Layered clothing is advisable due to potential changes in weather and elevation. A GPS device or a downloaded Komoot map is highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Is there food or accommodation available near the trailhead in Saldias?
faqs.answer: Saldias is a small village, and while it may have limited amenities, you can often find local establishments offering food and potentially accommodation. For a wider range of options, you might need to look in larger towns within the Navarra region. It's best to check in advance, especially during off-peak seasons.
